review_title: Aha! is an excellent product management system
description: A very user-friendly and straightforward interface. It's fantastic that a single platform can be used to oversee everything from product to service management to strategic planning to operational reporting.
author: Brandon E.
pros: A fantastic tool for project managers to use as a roadmap organizer. The ideas are fantastic, and the integration with Jira at the tactical level of engineering is functioning smoothly. Additional sophisticated features, such as various business modules, aid in the strategic direction of PM.
cons: It's odd that there aren't any external roadmap samples or case studies available from the makers of one of the greatest roadmap products in the world to aid with the design of your personal external roadmap.

review_title: Aha is great for Project Management
description: Overall I love using Aha, because as a project manager, I can prepare documentation, that I can customize
author: Verified Reviewer
pros: my information is secure and the customer support is great. I can manage projects easily and customize templates.
cons: I cannot speak to the Collaboration features as I have not had the opportunity to use them as yet. and in as much as it is value for money, I think it could be cheaper

review_title: Don't recommend. Read review for details..
author: Raf A.
pros: Cool logo. Good space to house personas and competitor analysis.
cons: In the initial onboarding process I would contact customer support for clarity, seeing as how I've never used such a software. I had one phone call, fairly short. I was later tasked with sole responsibility for the software - evangelizing and coaching others on how to use it. When I reached out to support for more clarity, the reps did EVERY possible not to get on a call with me. They would email me back every other day - directing me to instructional videos - refusing to get on the phone. I needed to integrate with Jira, "Please HELP!!??"- guess what? "We have some videos that can help you. Awful support. As well, not very user friendly, not customizable, and just down right inferior cosmetically.

review_title: Great customer support!
author: Cheri S.
pros: The customer support is quick to respond and really helpful! Even if I'm asking for a feature that doesn't exist yet, there's usually an idea that addresses my inquiry that support will provide. They will also ask for my end goal if it's unclear. This allows them to fully understand my need and provide any available workarounds. Their answers also usually include screenshots of steps that I need to take in order to accomplish my ask. Providing instructions in this way allows me to quickly execute their suggested path forward.
cons: There are features that are missing or that do exist, but we're just finding out about a few months after go-live that seem like they should have been mentioned during implementation.
author_duration: Used the software for: Less than 6 months

review_title: Good, but makes us look bad
description: Overall experience is good but I think you have the wrong features behind the Ideas Advanced paywall
author: Dominic G.
pros: Good APIs Amazing support team Integration with all the right tools
cons: Portal visibility restrictions behind the Ideas Advanced paywall stops the tool being considered valuable by our clients. They perceive Aha! to be a low quality product and ask us if we would consider moving back to JIRA to manage their product Ideas. There's also the fact that users can share a view with unauthenticated users without realising they are doing that, with all the security concerns that brings.

review_title: Project Management Platform for the People
description: As a firm we all enjoy our time on Aha! It really does help with project management and convey our ideas while working remotely
author: Alex C.
pros: I really enjoy the amount of project collaboration I can integrate on this platform. Aha! make it easy to allow for team collaboration on various mediums, but also makes it easy for all generations to use it.
cons: It feels a little dated, however this really does help when it comes to integrating all ages to use the platform.

review_title: Aha: a must have for Product Management
description: Aha is by far one of the best software on the market (if not the best) to manage a product, from small sized products to very large. I'm using the product from many years now, and I was able to build a lot over it. Via customization I can adapt the workflow and the data displayed in a very detailed manner. The integration with DevOps is providing almost everything which is needed, and what is missing I was able to integrate it developing on the API framework of Aha which is very complete.
author: Nicola R.
pros: The customization options (especially if you have an Enterprise+ plan) and the APIs available make it possible to really do everything. Support is very good, they answer in a good time and the answers are always able to solve the problems.
cons: Sometimes some APIs could be extended, but they are constantly evolving and usually you can get what you need in a relatively short time.

review_title: My Great Journey with Aha! in Product Management
author: Dilak S.
pros: Aha! makes it super easy to create visual roadmaps to outline critical business milestones in real-time.
cons: For over three years now, I have not yet come across issues while using Aha!.

review_title: Aha! Is an ideas portal that centralized our feedback in a well structured way.
description: Overall, myself and my team have had a great experience with AHA! And plan to continue utilizing it for collecting customer feedback and ideas.
author: Verified Reviewer
pros: Aha! Is a centralized place for our company to collect feedback and organize ideas. Having multiple products across our company can result in lots of data and ideas - AHA! Helps us centralize this.
cons: I think the UI/UX could use some work, but other than that it has been very good in providing what we need.

review_title: Gets the job done
author: Brazola M.
pros: Some pros for Aha! software are great customer support, very helpful and responsive. I also like how you can customize your roadmap just the way you need it.
cons: Some cons for Aha! software a steep learning curve. It can become overwhelming learning how to customize everything to your needs, and it is one of the more expensive softwares I’ve used.

review_title: Never going back to Jira
description: Fantastic overall. So impressed with the product, as well as the service. Would definitely recommend.
author: Mike G.
pros: Very powerful software, and almost no learning curve compared to Jira. So much more intuitive despite having a ton of advanced capabilities. I especially love how easy it is to share information with non-users, without having to manually update spreadsheets or slide decks.
cons: The Roadmaps pricing is a bit steep, but the Develop pricing is affordable enough that it makes up for it.

review_title: Using Aha for Education Assessment products
description: We are introducing the product management function accross the business. Therefore introducing the departments and senior management to the functions of Aha has been my priority. The multiple benefits have included, multi person access as a reviewer / viewer. Transparency of the portfolio roadmap, clarity and confirmation of epic and feature details and the development priorities. A prime benefit has been the capabilityof the tech team and CTO to cost up the features and epics in more detail than previously, supporting investment requests.
author: Karen R.
pros: For my role as Head of Product Management: The strategic view of the product portfolio is all important for the company to see and for me to report. The functions I use most are the strategic and portfolio roadmaps, goals and linked initiatives. I absolutely love the dashboard function, especially for executive resporting. And now use the different workspaces for the different departments who are dependant on the product management scheduling, this is also an added bonus.
cons: I really wish the functions would allow clarity for Strategic objectives, rather than goals and support the framing of dependant team and individual OKR's. I also find it difficult to delete releases and features on the Strategic level workspace. It would benefit the different types of users to have the functionalities that they need and navigate to it easily. From my role as head of to the associate product manager, we have different uses and feature requirements.

review_title: Aha support and documentation second to none. All the features you need.
description: A very positive experience that allows us to map strategic goals to the work done. It provides tools such as scorecards, custom fields and calculations that allow managers to assess the work on an even playing field, and set a roadmap that can be tracked with achievements building to the end goal.
author: Nicole D.
pros: Easy to integrate with development software and link to that work to the strategic goals and initiatives to make sure we build the right things the right way, in the right order for the right reasons. Achievements build and are tracked overtime to show the ROI quickly and easily.
cons: The price per user prohibits it from becoming the main tool used by all staff. It requires project tracking to be done in the development tool to minimise project costs overall.

review_title: The Best End-to-End Product Management Software Available
description: I have implemented and used Aha! across several companies and have seen it grow for nearly a decade. It keeps only getting better.
author: Matt G.
pros: Aha!'s data model is robust and flexible to account for our unique way of running our business. The reporting and notes/collaboration tools allow us to consolidate all of our PM-based efforts into a single system. This helps keep everyone aligned, organized and efficient.
cons: Because the application is so robust, there are some workflows sub-optimal user experiences (e.g. accessing views and report versions, creating capacity plans) that make onboarding new users cumbersome.

review_title: This tool has become critical to my success as an executive
author: Verified Reviewer
pros: The ability to connect strategy with plans and work being done towards business objectives.
cons: It takes some time to setup and understand but well worth the upfront effort
author_profession: Head of IT and Digital Experience

review_title: Amazing Support - Ideas portal capability which is easy to use and feature rich
description: Evaluated Aha and primary competitors, this was an ideal solution for us, with great private portal capability, and staff proxy voting. The support during the trial was exceptional, well beyond that of the others we were evaluating.
author: Steve M.
pros: Amazing support with well thought our Ideas portal capability, which was easy to setup and deploy.
cons: No cons thus far. We imported users, emailed went out and client engaged with assistance.
author_duration: Used the software for: Less than 6 months

review_title: Aha is the choice for power users
description: Overall I love it. I've moved more and more of my companies planning and roadmapping discipline into Aha and will continue pushing for more use of the tool.
author: Mike W.
pros: The ability to customize almost all aspects of Aha make it the choice for power users. If I can imagine it, I can probably do it. And when I can't figure out how to do it, Aha's team of professionals are there to help you out every time. The company isn't sitting on their laurels either, the engagement with customers on the Ideas portal is very active and features are constantly being added and improved.
cons: Integration with other tools can be complex to set up, or missing for many tools. Jira integration is very important for my company, but it doesn't always have the capabilities I'd like. I'd also like to see better integration with collaboration tools like Slack, LucidSpark and PowerPoint. I realize Aha is building competitive tools for most of those, but they are not, as of yet, mature enough to make the switch to using them.

review_title: Aha as a product management tool
description: Great tool, very nice UI, excellent support!
author: DARYNA P.
pros: Great functionality covering different angles of product management
cons: Automation section has a lot to be improved

review_title: Game changing software for product teams
description: Very satisfying, this tool has a chance to help us transform as a business!
author: Matt T.
pros: Very initiative to use and can be extremely powerful if setup with some foresight. It is flexible enough to be tailored to any kind of business yet rigid enough to instill needed discipline when using across a team or teams..
cons: Not much to dislike. While it can feel expensive, their reviewer and viewer (free users) make it easy to scale across many team members.

review_title: Convenient Tool for Product Managers, Aha!
description: Aha! is no doubt a sophisticated tool for driving product strategy, sharing your roadmap, and detailing features. It's very flexible and configurable so it requires a little while to get fluent with everything it may possibly do, and be aware of the best workflow patterns with it. That said, once you discover your groove it gets much more easily. Pricing is also in the premium end of the market, particularly for smaller companies, but you do tend to obtain what you pay off.
author: Ajinkya J.
pros: Aha! offers a comprehensive range of strategies for managing the entire product management and marketing cycle. I began using product development tools like Jira and then VersionOne, but they both appeared and functioned as if they were developed on purpose and eventually evolved into product management tools. None of them were in charge of product marketing. It was clear from the moment a fellow CTO introduced me to Aha! that this tool was explicitly designed to handle the full scope of product management. The governance features that assist product owners in focusing on the business plan and priorities also aid in the definition of product strategies to achieve those objectives. Customer personas assist the development team in remaining customer-centric. Our product owners enjoy working with feature development because they get to decide both how the features should function and how feature delivery should be prioritised. The Aha! Score, which can be customised, helps to balance value with development costs. Contributors are encouraged to request functionality, and the review process ensures that the promotion (or lack thereof) of a concept to a feature is transparent. Executive management and the board of directors expect product roadmaps to include information in an accessible format and Aha! has delivered efficiently so far.
cons: The tool is extremely effective, with numerous features that make it simple to build a complex puzzle that occupies all of your time, causing you to lose track of the original goal: efficiently produce a product that meets consumer standards and business objectives. Keeping the model easy necessitates discipline. Aha! is so powerful and so customizable that it took me a while to figure out how best to apply it to my particular situations and challenges. That said, their amazing customer success team has been and continues to work patiently and diligently to help me make the most out of it.

review_title: Used Aha ! to communicate back with my Product managers
author: Verified Reviewer
pros: I like the separation of ideas and features, compared to other tools in which all feature suggestions are created in the same way, in aha features are first suggested as IDEAs and the PM promotes them. It was very easy to track features that were promoted and getting notified for features being too long in the backlog.
cons: The integration to Salesforce was bad, I ended up creating ideas directly instead of creating them from salesforce as I should have as a CSM. Furthermore, the main drawback of Aha is that you don't manage bugs or development in it unlike SCM tools or Jira (bitBucket) which makes Aha! an expensive tool since it's used only for PMs and there is a limit to the number of dashboards one can track when my developers managed bugs and code in Git.

review_title: Aha offers tremendous value for product managers and integrates well with development software.
description: I've really enjoyed using it and continue to find new ways I can leverage its features. It's worth mentioning that when I have had questions, or something has gone wrong their technical support has been phenomenal. They respond in the same day and in most cases are able to immediately answer my questions. In cases, where an actual bug was found, I've even had their developers fix the issue within a couple days and let me know to try it again.
author: Scott W.
pros: The product has great tools for defining your product strategy, vision, mission, and allows you to easily track ideas, features, and plan your releases effectively. It offers a great integration with development software such as Azure Devops or Jira to bridge the gap between product management and development. I've found the software easy to learn and intuitive as I never had formal training and was still able to figure out how to use it well.
cons: I haven't experienced any feature that I've found troublesome or largely lacking in an essential capability as it is customizable in the right places. There are some features (i.e. polls) that I don't have access to with the current license that we have that I would like to try out so I can't say for sure if there are any gaps in such features.

review_title: Aha is a great way to manage the product portfolio
description: Aha provides a place to capture our entire portfolio in one place and allows independent support of each business division. This is enabling enhanced planning dialogue and greater transparency throughout our organization. We use it to model and support our value stream which makes it easy to ascertain current information about the disposition of each feature. This, in turn, informs better decision making. Also, on the rare occasion when we do need support, the team at Aha is always responsive and always very helpful. And the tool is always available and stable and has not left us hanging at any critical moments
author: Matt Z.
pros: the flexibility/configurability is the best thing about Aha. It can be configured to match business processes and needs and does not force adherence to any particular process. The diverse needs across our entire business portfolio are supported.
cons: understanding "the lay of the land" within the tool is always a little confusing for my new users. Some expect to have hand-holding type navigation, but that is not this tool. A few minutes to show them the basics usually gets them up and running.

review_title: Aha for Roadmap delivery
description: Overall experience using Aha is very good, we are easily able to manage the roadmap items and can generate the report after end of FY.
author: Gouri S.
pros: I liked the multiple features about Aha software, mentioning below - 1. Easy task creation in form of epics 2. Drag facility is available for prioritisation 3. Easy to Use
cons: There are some features which can be improved. i.e. navigation between multiple product roadmap can be smoother, and giant chart can be managed in a better way.

review_title: A Product Manager's best friend
author: Simon M.
pros: Having been on a long journey over the years with many different types of project/product management software solutions, none of them ever seemed to be "the one". Aha takes a different approach, and focuses on management of end-customer value that is aligned against the vision you have for your company and its products. It's the first work management platform I've used that took this holistic approach, and actually its massively empowering. I'm now able to construct high level roadmaps for complex projects that span multi-discipline teams that can be reported to all stakeholders very clearly and easily. Managing the features the team are going to focus on in a customer centric way is also great. Yes, you can use Aha for task management, but often in a product management capacity it's more important to express WHAT customer outcomes & benefits we want, and then let the team figure out HOW to deliver them. Moreover, the customer support from the Aha team is excellent, with plenty of guides not just how to use the platform, but also how to strategically tackle typical challenges that product managers face. I highly recommend Aha. It's a liberating product from a team that clearly have an awesome culture based around customer success.
cons: Aha is a sophisticated tool that's very flexible and configurable so it takes a little while to get fluent with everything it can do, and understand the best workflow patterns with it. That said, once you find your groove it gets much easier. The support team help with that though. Pricing is also in the premium end of the market, particularly for smaller companies, but then you do tend to get what you pay for.
